meta.name: Soul Food Cornbread (Skillet)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Classic cast-iron skillet cornbread with crisp edges. Lightly sweet option included.

meta.summary: Crisp-edged cast-iron cornbread with a tender crumb—perfect for potlikker, chili, or a holiday table.

notes: Quick breads are typically done around 190–200°F internal temperature.

ingredients.label: Bacon fat or butter (for preheating skillet)

steps.title: Preheat oven and skillet
steps.body: Place cast iron skillet in the oven and preheat to 425°F. Let the skillet heat fully while you mix the batter.

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
steps.body: In a bowl, whisk together cornmeal, flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and sugar (if using).

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients
steps.body: In a second bowl, whisk buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter until combined.

steps.title: Combine batter (do not overmix)
steps.body: Pour wet ingredients into dry ingredients. Stir just until no dry streaks remain. Batter will be thick—avoid overmixing.

steps.title: Grease the hot skillet
steps.body: Carefully remove hot skillet from oven. Add bacon fat or butter and swirl to coat bottom and sides.

steps.title: Bake until golden
steps.body: Pour batter into hot greased skillet (it should sizzle). Bake 18–24 minutes, until deeply golden and a toothpick comes out clean. Internal temp should be ~190–200°F.

steps.title: Rest briefly, then serve
steps.body: Rest 5–10 minutes. Slice and serve warm (great with collard greens potlikker or chili).
